behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is at a module boundary: inventory availability filtering must be applied correctly before/with ranking. A unit test of only ranking or only filtering can miss a real wiring bug where high-score out-of-stock items leak through.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation pipeline with a seeded catalog containing high-relevance in-stock and out-of-stock items. Verify final returned list contains no out-of-stock product IDs, including items that would otherwise rank at the top.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on handoff from user-settings data to filtering logic. Unit-testing filter logic alone can miss schema/serialization/mapping issues in blocked-category settings.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with blocked categories in settings storage, run recommendation generation, and assert no returned product belongs to blocked categories.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is core ranking-rule behavior inside the algorithm, testable at one function/object boundary with deterministic fixtures. No required external I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Call the ranking function with identical candidate products except browsing-signal features; assert products with recent-browsing match receive higher scores/ranks than no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is interaction between ranking output, sorting stability, and pagination module. Unit-testing pagination utility alone can miss non-stable ordering from upstream wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations from fixed data, request page 2 twice via the full local recommendation service path, and verify identical ordered IDs and correct offset/limit boundaries.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is an algorithmic constraint on ranked output composition; it can be validated at ranking/diversification function level with controlled inputs. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Provide candidate set and user history where all top relevance items are from seen categories; assert diversification logic includes at least one unseen-category product in final top-N.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when profile-state detection (cold-start) interacts with fallback retrieval (popularity source). Unit test of fallback scorer alone may not catch empty-path wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with no history in local stores, invoke recommendation generation, and assert non-empty results are ordered by popularity baseline.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on interaction between recommendation response, click event payload construction, and persistence/logging. Mocking persistence can hide field-mapping defects.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate user clicks on specific positions, and verify stored click records contain exact product ID, list position, and a valid event timestamp.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Performance threshold is sensitive to real runtime/OS/process behavior and cannot be trusted with mocked infra. Requires end-to-end execution timing in realistic environment.
behaviors.test_description: Run a performance test against a 100k-product dataset in a production-like runtime, issue recommendation requests, and assert p95 latency is under 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Requires state transition across modules: purchase event ingestion/update plus next recommendation ranking. Unit tests of rank penalty alone can miss event-to-feature propagation failures.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record a purchase for one recommended product, regenerate recommendations, and verify purchased product rank drops relative to pre-purchase result.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures often come from module interactions (unstable sort, random seed handling, cache/state effects) rather than a single pure function. No LLM or external API needed.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user state and catalog snapshot, invoke full recommendation generation twice, and assert identical ordered product IDs and scores across both responses.
